data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/28/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise Ahead of Interest Rate Decision, Big Tech Earnings
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose early Wednesday as traders anticipated the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and earnings from three major technology companies. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.86%, 0.33%, and 0.02%, respectively, at 3:27 a.m. EST on January 28.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three indexes closed mixed, with the Dow dropping 0.83%. Shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) plunged 19.6%, dragging down the index. UNH fell despite reporting robust Q4FY25 revenues, as news of a Medicare Advantage payment increase of just 0.09% for 2027 pressured the stock. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 gained 0.41% to finish at a record high, and the Nasdaq Composite climbed 0.91%.

On the economic front, all eyes are on the Federal Reserve's first interest-rate decision today. The central bank is widely expected to hold interest rates steady, while investors look for clues about future policy directions.

Turning to earnings season, major companies reporting today include Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), Tesla (TSLA), IBM (IBM), AT&T (T), and Starbucks (SBUX).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.23%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.48 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,290 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid expectations of a slew of corporate earnings releases.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 28, tracking the S&P 500's gains.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index added 2.43%. In China, the Shanghai Composite added 0.27%, while the Shenzhen Component rose 0.21%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 0.05%, while the Topix fell 0.79%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Delta Air Lines (DAL) Orders 31 New Airbus Jets to Expand Long-Haul Fleet
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) has ordered 31 new Airbus (EADSY) widebody jets to help modernize its international fleet and support expansion into key global markets. The order includes 16 A330‑900s and 15 A350‑900s, two of Airbus's most advanced long-range aircraft. Deliveries of the newly ordered aircraft are expected to begin in 2029.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new order strengthens Delta's long-standing partnership with Airbus. After these jets are delivered, Delta's widebody fleet will include 55 A330neos and 79 A350s, making it one of the largest Airbus widebody operators globally.

Push to Expand International Reach

Delta CEO Ed Bastian said the new aircraft will help the airline grow its international footprint and better serve long-haul markets. He noted that the jets will "enhance our capabilities and elevate our premium offerings."

Bastian added that Delta's partnership with Airbus continues to deliver operational efficiencies and long-term cost benefits.

The A350‑900 can fly up to 9,700 nautical miles, while the A330‑900 offers a range of 8,100 nautical miles, giving Delta the flexibility to launch and sustain long-distance routes.

Fuel Efficiency and Premium Experience

Both aircraft types deliver about 25% better fuel efficiency and lower emissions compared to older widebody models, an important factor as airlines make efforts to reduce costs and meet sustainability goals.

It is worth noting that Delta's incoming jets will feature upgraded premium cabins, including Delta One Suites, Delta Premium Select, and Delta Comfort+, along with seatback entertainment, high-speed Wi-Fi, and enhanced food and beverage options.

The focus on premium seating reflects rising demand from business and long-haul leisure travelers seeking more comfort on flights lasting 12 hours or more.

Is DAL Stock a Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, DAL stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 18 Buys assigned in the last three months. At $83.50, the average Delta Air Lines stock price target implies a 26.25% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Seagate Stock (STX) Soars on Solid AI-Led Q2 Beat and Outlook, Analysts Boost Price Targets
data.description:
Seagate Technology (STX) stock jumped about 11% in Wednesday's pre-market trading after the data storage provider reported better-than-expected results for the second quarter of Fiscal 2026 and issued a solid outlook. The company is seeing a surge in data center storage demand amid the ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) boom. Several analysts raised their price targets following Seagate's stellar results.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Seagate Impresses with Q2 Earnings and Robust Outlook

Seagate's Q2 FY26 revenue grew by 21.5% year-over-year to $2.83 billion, surpassing the Street's estimate of $2.74 billion. Moreover, adjusted earnings per share (EPS) surged by more than 53% to $3.11, exceeding the consensus estimate of $2.84. The AI wave has sparked robust demand for hard-disk drive (HDD) makers like Seagate and Western Digital (WDC).

Consequently, STX stock has rallied by an impressive 259% over the past year, while shares of Western Digital have jumped 423% over the same period.

Looking ahead, Seagate expects Q3 FY26 revenue in the range of $2.80 billion to $3 billion, above the consensus estimate of $2.78 billion. Also, adjusted EPS is estimated to be between $3.20 and $3.60, compared to the Street's forecast of $2.99.

Analysts Raise STX Stock Price Targets

In reaction to the Q2 FY26 print, Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan raised his price target for Seagate Technology stock to $450 from $400 and reiterated a Buy rating. The 5-star analyst noted STX's solid earnings beat and guidance, with gross and operating margin outlook beating the Street's estimates by 100 basis points.

Mohan added that management remains confident about achieving 50% of nearline exabytes shipments from Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording (HAMR) drives by the second half of calendar year 2026.  The analyst remains positive on STX stock based on robust AI and data center demand, tight supply-demand conditions supporting pricing, solid margins, and disciplined operating expenses, which should drive estimates higher. Overall, Mohan remains bullish on STX stock, given secular cloud demand, revenue and margin expansion, and a clear path to higher-capacity HAMR HDDs.

Additionally, Morgan Stanley analyst Erik Woodring raised his price target for STX stock to $468 from $372 and reiterated a Buy rating. The analyst noted that Seagate's metrics were strong despite elevated expectations. Woodring highlighted that HDDs continue to gain from surging data center storage demand (video, agentic AI, multi-modal, checkpointing, and more), higher pricing, and a mix shift to higher-capacity HDDs that carry premium margins.

The 5-star analyst added that at the micro level, Seagate's HAMR rollout is picking up pace, with over 1.5 million shipments of the Mozaic 3 platform this quarter, up from about 1 million last quarter. In fact, all major U.S. cloud service providers (CSPs) are now qualified for Mozaic 3, with a growing number of CSPs qualifying for Mozaic 4. Management expects the Mozaic 4 ramp to strengthen in the second half of 2026. Woodring raised his FY27 estimates by 25% and said, "We can still find healthy upside in our model, primarily on pricing and gross margins."

Is STX a Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts at Citi, Barclays, and Mizuho also raised their estimates for Seagate. Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Seagate Technology stock based on 14 Buys and four Holds. The average STX stock price target of $364.53 indicates 2% downside risk.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Here's Why Iren Stock Jumped 15% and What Wall Street Sees Next
data.description:
Following Monday's pullback after rival CoreWeave (CRVW) announced a new $2 billion deal with Nvidia (NVDA), AI cloud company and bitcoin miner Iren Limited's (IREN) shares jumped 14.57% on Tuesday. The rally appears driven by continued investor confidence in AI firms, even as Anthropic looks to double its fundraising target to $20 billion for a $350 billion valuation.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In 2025, AI startups collectively raised a record $150 billion, led by impressive funding rounds by OpenAI and Anthropic. This brings the number close to the $172 billion raised in initial public offerings (IPOs) last year despite the 39% year-over-year jump in IPOs, according to EY data.

Iren's AI-Fueled Rally Continues

Recently, B. Riley analysts Nick Giles and Fedor Shabalin argued that Iren shares — which are traded as a proxy for AI stocks — would improve once sentiment on AI companies picks up again.

This has proven true as Iren shares have recorded various rallying periods in the last few weeks, including when TSMC (TSM) reported blockbuster fourth-quarter results that showed continued strong demand for its services, and the world's largest contract chip manufacturer stated plans to expand its capital expenditure from around $41 billion to between $52 billion and $56 billion in 2026.

Iren also notched a 13% gain earlier this month, coinciding with leading AI firms rolling out significant artificial intelligence updates at the CES 2026 international trade show in Las Vegas, Nevada.

Where Is Iren Stock Heading Next?

On Wall Street, most analysts remain Bullish on Iren even as the data center operator's shares have soared nearly 60% since the start of the year.

Meanwhile, Bernstein (AB) recently maintained IREN as its top AI pick for the year. In addition, H.C. Wainwright analyst Mike Colonnese has argued that Iren "is set for a transformative year," with the possibility to finally earn revenue upside from its recently signed $9.7 billion deal with Microsoft (MSFT).

Is IREN Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Iren Limited's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on the analyst consensus rating. This breaks down to nine Buys, two Holds, and one Sell issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

Moreover, the average IREN price target of $82.80 suggests about 38% growth potential.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why AI Chip Stocks Are Rising in Pre-Market Today
data.description:
AI chip stocks are rising in premarket trading on Wednesday, driven by Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 chip approval from China and strong results from chipmakers ASML Holding (ASML) and SK Hynix, reflecting robust demand for semiconductors. As of this writing, Broadcom (AVGO) rose 2.4%, Micron (MU) gained 5.4%, AMD (AMD) added 2%, and NVDA was up 1.3%.

data.title: Tesla's FSD Adoption Could Surge Above 50%, Says Dan Ives
data.description:
Tesla's (TSLA) Full Self-Driving (FSD) software could see a sharp jump in adoption, according to Wedbush's five-star-rated analyst Dan Ives. In a recent note ahead of Tesla's Q4 earnings today, Ives said FSD penetration could rise from around 12% today to more than 50%, adding that the company remains on the verge of a robotaxi-driven transformation.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla is set to report its Q4 2025 earnings after the market closes today. Wall Street expects earnings of $0.46 per share, down from $0.73 in the same quarter last year. Revenue is projected at $24.8 billion, compared with $25.7 billion a year earlier. Ives added that the upcoming earnings call will focus heavily on Tesla's Robotaxi rollout across the U.S., including plans to remove safety drivers from its fleet. He also expects an aggressive Robotaxi rollout across more than 30 U.S. cities in 2026.

Is Tesla on Track for $3T Valuation?

Ives, a long-time Tesla bull, believes rising FSD adoption and advances in autonomy could unlock up to $1 trillion in value, putting Tesla on a path toward a $2 trillion to $3 trillion market cap in 2026.

However, his latest note does not spell out how Tesla would close that gap, whether through pricing changes, bundled offerings, or a shift in customer behavior. While Tesla recently announced that it is moving away from one-time FSD purchases in favor of subscriptions, that change alone is unlikely to lift adoption from around 12% to 50%.

Ives Stays Bullish on Tesla's AI Progress

Ives treats Tesla's progress on robotaxis as a given, pointing to the removal of safety drivers in Austin as a major turning point. Additionally, he suggested that President Trump could issue an executive order on autonomous driving, clearing regulatory hurdles and quickly boosting FSD adoption.

On Wall Street, Ives has the Street-high price target of $600 for TSLA stock, predicting an upside of almost 40% from current levels. Overall, he believes investors are beginning to look beyond vehicle deliveries. Instead, Tesla's long-term growth story is increasingly centered on AI, FSD technology, Optimus robots, and robotaxis.

Is Tesla a Good Share to Buy?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a neutral stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 10 Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the last three months. The average price target for Tesla shares is $406.87, suggesting a potential downside of 5.6% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: BABA, TCEHY: Why Are Chinese Tech Stocks Climbing Today?
data.description:
China tech stocks moved higher on Wednesday after Reuters reported that Beijing has approved purchases of Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 AI chips by some of the country's largest internet companies. ai-chips-clouding-stock-outlook">The report said Chinese authorities gave approval to Alibaba (BABA), Tencent (TCEHY), and ByteDance to buy more than 400,000 H200 chips in total. Other companies are expected to apply for approvals later, according to people familiar with the matter.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, the approvals come with conditions that are still being decided, and some may be restrictive.

Investors see the move as positive for China's biggest tech firms, which are spending heavily on data centers to build AI services. The H200 chips are reported to be far faster than the H20, making them important for training AI models at large tech firms.

China has encouraged companies to rely more on domestic chips, but regulators appear willing to allow limited imports to avoid slowing AI development at major internet platforms.

data.title: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) Sees 314% Usage Growth as 2026 Plans Take Shape
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) shared new updates that point to rising customer use and steady product gains across its quantum systems. The company said use of its Advantage2 annealing systems rose 314% over the past year, signaling stronger demand from paying customers. In the meantime, D-Wave continues to expand its software tools that blend quantum and classical computing in real business tasks.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Speaking at its Qubits 2026 user event, the company highlighted new hybrid solver updates that let customers add machine learning models into optimization work. According to D-Wave, these tools help users solve large planning and pricing problems that can be hard for classical systems alone. Chief Development Officer Trevor Lanting said the goal is to help customers solve complex problems now while building future systems that scale.

On Tuesday, QBTS shares rose 3.96% to close at $24.69.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Anthropic Set to Raise $20B at a Stunning $350B Valuation
data.description:
Anthropic, one of the fastest‑growing AI start-ups, is preparing to raise around $20 billion from venture capital firms and other major investors, the Financial Times reported. The deal would value the company at a stunning $350 billion. Interestingly, Anthropic initially sought around $10 billion to support its expansion plans, but strong investor interest pushed the total to double that amount.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company is expected to secure an initial $10 billion to $15 billion as early as Tuesday, with the remainder to be completed in the coming weeks.

The round is being led by Singapore's sovereign wealth fund GIC and U.S. investment firm Coatue. Sequoia Capital is also expected to make a significant commitment. Existing backers, including Iconiq Capital, Altimeter Capital, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Menlo Ventures, G Squared, and Factorial Funds, are likely to participate as well.

Anthropic Eyes an IPO as AI Adoption Grows

Importantly, the fundraising comes as Anthropic prepares for a potential initial public offering (IPO), which could take place later this year. The company has hired law firm Wilson Sonsini to begin early IPO preparations and has held preliminary discussions with banks about a future listing.

Anthropic, founded by former OpenAI researchers, has built a strong reputation for its focus on "constitutional AI," a method aimed at making large language models safer and more predictable. Its Claude family of models has gained traction with enterprises looking for powerful AI tools.

The company has also won support from major tech giants such as Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOGL), which have each signed multibillion-dollar deals to bring Anthropic's AI models into their cloud platforms.

Overall, a $350 billion valuation would reflect a huge jump from Anthropic's earlier funding rounds and points to competition among investors to secure stakes in leading AI companies.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: SK Hynix Inc. Reports Record Earnings on AI Memory Growth
data.description:
SK Hynix Inc. (HK:7709) just posted its strongest quarter on record, showing how fast AI demand is reshaping the memory market. Operating profit more than doubled to ₩19.2 trillion, while revenue rose to ₩32.8 trillion, both well ahead of forecasts. The results pushed shares higher and reinforced the company's lead in high-bandwidth memory used in AI systems.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Once known for sharp cycles, memory is now seeing steadier gains. AI servers need far more memory than past systems, and supply remains tight. As one fund manager put it, "There are no easy wins and levers from a supply perspective to meet this new demand driver." As a result, buyers are willing to pay more, and pricing power has shifted back to chipmakers.

AI Memory Lifts the Sector

SK Hynix is the top supplier of advanced memory to Nvidia (NVDA), whose AI chips dominate data centers. It is also reported to be the sole supplier of this memory for a custom AI chip used by Microsoft (MSFT). At the same time, SK Hynix plans to cancel about $8.6 billion worth of treasury shares, signaling confidence in cash flow and demand.

Meanwhile, this shift is affecting the wider chip space. Micron Technology (MU) is also benefiting from rising prices for memory used in AI servers. In contrast, Intel (INTC) is still working to regain momentum in data center chips, where AI spending favors newer designs and faster memory links.

Importantly, more capacity is going to AI memory, which limits the output of standard chips. That tight supply is lifting prices across the board. Analysts expect average selling prices for DRAM to rise about 120% this year, with NAND prices up about 90%.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to align all stocks mentioned in the text with other smartphone makers and chip manufacturers, which are also affected by the current landscape.
data.author: Ran Melamed
